title: centrosema tapirapoanense
intro: Centrosema tapirapoanense, also known as the 'poor cow', is a species of legume in the family Fabaceae. It is a perennial plant native to the savannas of South America, particularly in Brazil. The medicinal properties of Centrosema tapirapoanense include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve kidney function, and act as an antiseptic. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat kidney stones, improve urinary function, and reduce the risk of kidney disease, and it can be consumed as a tea, capsule, or infused in water for its herbal preparations.
